6-Day Workout Plan

Warm-Up · Train · Cool-Down · Every Session

Age 45 Weight 160 lbs Height 5'10" Goal Muscle + Definition
Freak Athlete GHD · Barbell + Plates · Dumbbells · Kettlebell · Pilates Reformer · Quadro Push-Up Board · Parallettes · Balance Board · Ab Roller
Monday
Chest & Triceps
Quadro board + parallettes added for push-up depth and variety. Rest 60–90 sec on main work.
⚡ Warm-Up — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Arm Circles + Shoulder Rolls
WARM-UP
60 sec each direction
Big circles forward then backward. Loosens the shoulder capsule before any pressing or pulling. Never skip this at 45.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Torso Rotation
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Arms out to sides, rotate upper body left and right. Wakes up the thoracic spine and core. Critical before back and shoulder days.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Knee Circles + Hip Opener
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Hands on knees, circle outward then inward. Then hip circles. Gets blood into the joints before any lower body work.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Inchworm
WARM-UP
5–6 reps
Stand, fold forward, walk hands out to plank, walk feet to hands, stand. Full body activation — hamstrings, core, shoulders all in one movement.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Bodyweight Squat
WARM-UP
10 reps
Slow controlled squats, full depth. Activates glutes, quads, and warms the hips. Add a pause at the bottom.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Mountain Climbers
WARM-UP
20 reps (10 each leg)
Plank position, drive knees to chest alternating. Gets heart rate up and activates the core before training.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Warm-Up Rule: Never skip this. At 45 your joints and connective tissue need 5 minutes to get synovial fluid moving. Cold training is how injuries happen. Total time: ~5 min.
Main Work
Start
Finish
Barbell/DB Bench Press
4 sets × 8–10 reps
Use barbell for maximum load. 2 sec descent, drive hard. Primary chest builder.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Incline Dumbbell Press
3 sets × 10 reps
Bench ~45°. Upper chest for that full defined look. Elbows at 45° to torso.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Quadro Board — Wide Position (Chest)
PUSH-UP BOARD
3 sets × 12 reps
Use the red/chest position on your Quadro board. Wider than shoulder width — maximizes chest stretch and activation. Keeps wrists neutral.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Parallette Push-Up
PARALLETTES
3 sets × max reps
Place parallette handles on floor, push-up with increased range of motion — chest drops below hand level for a deeper stretch than any floor push-up. Game changer for chest development.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Overhead DB Tricep Extension
3 sets × 12 reps
Two hands on one dumbbell. Elbows point forward. Long head of tricep.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Bench Dips
3 sets × 15 reps
Hands on bench behind you, feet forward, dip down until upper arms parallel to floor. Pure tricep definition builder.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Quadro Board — Tricep Position
PUSH-UP BOARD
3 sets × max reps
Narrow yellow/tricep position on Quadro board. Elbows track straight back. Finisher — go to failure.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
❄️ Cool-Down — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Child's Pose
COOL-DOWN
60 sec hold
Kneel, sit back on heels, arms forward on floor. Decompresses the spine and stretches the lats and lower back after any pulling day. Breathe deeply.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
COOL-DOWN
30 sec each side
Lunge position, drop back knee to floor, push hips forward. Hip flexors tighten from sitting — stretch them daily. Especially important after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Groin and Inner Thigh Stretch
COOL-DOWN
45 sec hold
Wide stance, reach forward or side bend. Stretches the adductors and groin. Important for hip mobility and recovery after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Plank Hold
COOL-DOWN
30–45 sec
End every session with a plank. Resets core stability, teaches proper bracing, and is active recovery for the spine after heavy lifting.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Cool-Down Rule: Static stretches after training (not before). Hold each stretch — don't bounce. This is when flexibility actually improves and when your nervous system downregulates. Total time: ~5 min.
Tip: The parallette push-up is a game changer — deeper range of motion means more chest stretch and more growth. Start with knees if full depth is too hard.
Tuesday
Back
Reformer row added for controlled lat work with zero spinal compression. Rest 75–90 sec.
⚡ Warm-Up — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Arm Circles + Shoulder Rolls
WARM-UP
60 sec each direction
Big circles forward then backward. Loosens the shoulder capsule before any pressing or pulling. Never skip this at 45.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Torso Rotation
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Arms out to sides, rotate upper body left and right. Wakes up the thoracic spine and core. Critical before back and shoulder days.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Knee Circles + Hip Opener
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Hands on knees, circle outward then inward. Then hip circles. Gets blood into the joints before any lower body work.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Inchworm
WARM-UP
5–6 reps
Stand, fold forward, walk hands out to plank, walk feet to hands, stand. Full body activation — hamstrings, core, shoulders all in one movement.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Bodyweight Squat
WARM-UP
10 reps
Slow controlled squats, full depth. Activates glutes, quads, and warms the hips. Add a pause at the bottom.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Mountain Climbers
WARM-UP
20 reps (10 each leg)
Plank position, drive knees to chest alternating. Gets heart rate up and activates the core before training.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Warm-Up Rule: Never skip this. At 45 your joints and connective tissue need 5 minutes to get synovial fluid moving. Cold training is how injuries happen. Total time: ~5 min.
Main Work
Start
Finish
Bent-Over Barbell Row
4 sets × 8–10 reps
Hinge at hips, back flat, bar to lower chest. Primary back mass builder.
▶ YouTube Rest 90 sec
Start
Finish
One-Arm Dumbbell Row
3 sets × 10–12 reps each
Knee on bench, flat back. Pull elbow past hip. Squeeze at top.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Reformer Row (Foot Strap Pull)
REFORMER
3 sets × 12 reps
Sit facing the foot bar on your reformer, use the straps to row toward your hips. Spring resistance is excellent for controlled lat activation with less spinal load than barbell.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Renegade Row
3 sets × 8 reps each side
Plank on dumbbells, row one arm at a time. Back and core simultaneously.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
GHD Hyperextension
GHD
4 sets × 12–15 reps
Lock feet in Freak Athlete GHD. Hinge at hips, squeeze glutes hard at top. Lower back and erector builder.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
DB Shrug
3 sets × 15 reps
Straight up and down, pause 1 sec at top. Upper trap thickness.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Seated Rear Delt Raise
3 sets × 15 reps
Lean forward, light DBs, raise to sides. Makes shoulders look complete from every angle.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
❄️ Cool-Down — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Child's Pose
COOL-DOWN
60 sec hold
Kneel, sit back on heels, arms forward on floor. Decompresses the spine and stretches the lats and lower back after any pulling day. Breathe deeply.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
COOL-DOWN
30 sec each side
Lunge position, drop back knee to floor, push hips forward. Hip flexors tighten from sitting — stretch them daily. Especially important after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Groin and Inner Thigh Stretch
COOL-DOWN
45 sec hold
Wide stance, reach forward or side bend. Stretches the adductors and groin. Important for hip mobility and recovery after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Plank Hold
COOL-DOWN
30–45 sec
End every session with a plank. Resets core stability, teaches proper bracing, and is active recovery for the spine after heavy lifting.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Cool-Down Rule: Static stretches after training (not before). Hold each stretch — don't bounce. This is when flexibility actually improves and when your nervous system downregulates. Total time: ~5 min.
Tip: The reformer row on back day is great because the spring resistance keeps tension throughout the entire range of motion — something dumbbells can't do.
Wednesday
Arms
Parallette L-sit and Quadro board variations added. High volume, moderate weight. Rest 45–60 sec.
⚡ Warm-Up — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Arm Circles + Shoulder Rolls
WARM-UP
60 sec each direction
Big circles forward then backward. Loosens the shoulder capsule before any pressing or pulling. Never skip this at 45.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Torso Rotation
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Arms out to sides, rotate upper body left and right. Wakes up the thoracic spine and core. Critical before back and shoulder days.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Knee Circles + Hip Opener
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Hands on knees, circle outward then inward. Then hip circles. Gets blood into the joints before any lower body work.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Inchworm
WARM-UP
5–6 reps
Stand, fold forward, walk hands out to plank, walk feet to hands, stand. Full body activation — hamstrings, core, shoulders all in one movement.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Bodyweight Squat
WARM-UP
10 reps
Slow controlled squats, full depth. Activates glutes, quads, and warms the hips. Add a pause at the bottom.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Mountain Climbers
WARM-UP
20 reps (10 each leg)
Plank position, drive knees to chest alternating. Gets heart rate up and activates the core before training.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Warm-Up Rule: Never skip this. At 45 your joints and connective tissue need 5 minutes to get synovial fluid moving. Cold training is how injuries happen. Total time: ~5 min.
Main Work
Start
Finish
Concentration Curl
3 sets × 12 reps each
Elbow on inner thigh, squeeze hard at top, slow negative. Best for bicep peak.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
Start
Finish
Incline Dumbbell Curl
3 sets × 10 reps each
Arms hang back on incline. Pre-stretches the long head for fuller development.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
21s — Dumbbell Curl
3 sets (7+7+7)
7 lower → 7 upper → 7 full. Drop weight 20–30% from normal curl.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Hammer Curl
3 sets × 12 reps each
Neutral grip. Hits brachialis — makes bicep look bigger.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Parallette L-Sit Hold
PARALLETTES
3 sets × 10–20 sec hold
Place parallettes on floor, support your weight on straight arms, tuck knees or extend legs. Builds triceps, core, and shoulder stability simultaneously. Work up to 20 sec holds.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Lying Dumbbell Skull Crusher
3 sets × 12 reps
Lower DBs toward temples, upper arms stay still. Thick lateral tricep head.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Quadro Board — Shoulder Position (Pike)
PUSH-UP BOARD
3 sets × 10 reps
Use the red shoulder position. Get into a pike/downward dog position and do angled push-ups. Hits the front delt and tricep differently than flat push-ups.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Tricep Kickback Finisher
3 sets × 15 reps each
Light weight, full squeeze at extension. Burn it out.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
❄️ Cool-Down — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Child's Pose
COOL-DOWN
60 sec hold
Kneel, sit back on heels, arms forward on floor. Decompresses the spine and stretches the lats and lower back after any pulling day. Breathe deeply.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
COOL-DOWN
30 sec each side
Lunge position, drop back knee to floor, push hips forward. Hip flexors tighten from sitting — stretch them daily. Especially important after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Groin and Inner Thigh Stretch
COOL-DOWN
45 sec hold
Wide stance, reach forward or side bend. Stretches the adductors and groin. Important for hip mobility and recovery after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Plank Hold
COOL-DOWN
30–45 sec
End every session with a plank. Resets core stability, teaches proper bracing, and is active recovery for the spine after heavy lifting.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Cool-Down Rule: Static stretches after training (not before). Hold each stretch — don't bounce. This is when flexibility actually improves and when your nervous system downregulates. Total time: ~5 min.
Tip: The L-sit hold is deceptively hard — it builds the triceps and core at the same time and takes 0 equipment beyond your parallettes. Work up to 20 sec holds over time.
Thursday
Legs + GHD + Reformer
Your best equipped day. GHD, reformer, and balance board all used here. Rest 90 sec on compounds.
⚡ Warm-Up — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Arm Circles + Shoulder Rolls
WARM-UP
60 sec each direction
Big circles forward then backward. Loosens the shoulder capsule before any pressing or pulling. Never skip this at 45.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Torso Rotation
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Arms out to sides, rotate upper body left and right. Wakes up the thoracic spine and core. Critical before back and shoulder days.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Knee Circles + Hip Opener
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Hands on knees, circle outward then inward. Then hip circles. Gets blood into the joints before any lower body work.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Inchworm
WARM-UP
5–6 reps
Stand, fold forward, walk hands out to plank, walk feet to hands, stand. Full body activation — hamstrings, core, shoulders all in one movement.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Bodyweight Squat
WARM-UP
10 reps
Slow controlled squats, full depth. Activates glutes, quads, and warms the hips. Add a pause at the bottom.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Mountain Climbers
WARM-UP
20 reps (10 each leg)
Plank position, drive knees to chest alternating. Gets heart rate up and activates the core before training.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Warm-Up Rule: Never skip this. At 45 your joints and connective tissue need 5 minutes to get synovial fluid moving. Cold training is how injuries happen. Total time: ~5 min.
Main Work
Start
Finish
Barbell Back Squat
4 sets × 8–10 reps
Biggest compound movement. Drives testosterone and GH. Depth to parallel or below.
▶ YouTube Rest 90 sec
Start
Finish
Romanian Deadlift
3 sets × 10 reps
Hinge at hips, feel hamstring stretch before driving hips forward.
▶ YouTube Rest 90 sec
Start
Finish
Glute Ham Raise
GHD
3 sets × 6–10 reps
Lock feet in Freak Athlete GHD. Lower slowly using hamstrings, pull back up. The hardest hamstring exercise in existence.
▶ YouTube Rest 90 sec
Start
Finish
Reformer Leg Press — Footwork
REFORMER
3 sets × 15 reps
Feet on the foot bar of your reformer, push the carriage out and return. Spring resistance builds quads and calves with zero spinal load. Use heels, toes, and turned-out positions.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
GHD Hyperextension
GHD
3 sets × 15 reps
Hinge at hips, squeeze glutes at top. Lower back and glute builder.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
DB Reverse Lunge
3 sets × 10 reps each leg
Step back, knee hovers above floor. Knee-friendly quad and glute builder.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Reverse Hyperextension
GHD
3 sets × 15 reps
Lie face down on GHD bench, raise legs behind you. Decompresses spine and targets glutes and hamstrings.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Balance Board Single-Leg Stand
BALANCE BOARD
3 sets × 30 sec each leg
Stand on one leg on the wobble/balance disc. Builds ankle stability, proprioception, and the small stabilizer muscles in the knee and hip. Use the largest disc first.
▶ YouTube Rest 30 sec
Start
Finish
Standing Calf Raise
4 sets × 20 reps
Full range — all the way up, all the way down. Pause at top. Use DBs for load.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
❄️ Cool-Down — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Child's Pose
COOL-DOWN
60 sec hold
Kneel, sit back on heels, arms forward on floor. Decompresses the spine and stretches the lats and lower back after any pulling day. Breathe deeply.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
COOL-DOWN
30 sec each side
Lunge position, drop back knee to floor, push hips forward. Hip flexors tighten from sitting — stretch them daily. Especially important after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Groin and Inner Thigh Stretch
COOL-DOWN
45 sec hold
Wide stance, reach forward or side bend. Stretches the adductors and groin. Important for hip mobility and recovery after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Plank Hold
COOL-DOWN
30–45 sec
End every session with a plank. Resets core stability, teaches proper bracing, and is active recovery for the spine after heavy lifting.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Cool-Down Rule: Static stretches after training (not before). Hold each stretch — don't bounce. This is when flexibility actually improves and when your nervous system downregulates. Total time: ~5 min.
Tip: The balance board single-leg stand looks easy — it's not. The small stabilizer muscles it activates protect your knees and ankles and directly improve squat depth over time.
Friday
Shoulders
Quadro board pike push-up added as a shoulder-specific push-up variation. Rest 60 sec.
⚡ Warm-Up — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Arm Circles + Shoulder Rolls
WARM-UP
60 sec each direction
Big circles forward then backward. Loosens the shoulder capsule before any pressing or pulling. Never skip this at 45.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Torso Rotation
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Arms out to sides, rotate upper body left and right. Wakes up the thoracic spine and core. Critical before back and shoulder days.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Knee Circles + Hip Opener
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Hands on knees, circle outward then inward. Then hip circles. Gets blood into the joints before any lower body work.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Inchworm
WARM-UP
5–6 reps
Stand, fold forward, walk hands out to plank, walk feet to hands, stand. Full body activation — hamstrings, core, shoulders all in one movement.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Bodyweight Squat
WARM-UP
10 reps
Slow controlled squats, full depth. Activates glutes, quads, and warms the hips. Add a pause at the bottom.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Mountain Climbers
WARM-UP
20 reps (10 each leg)
Plank position, drive knees to chest alternating. Gets heart rate up and activates the core before training.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Warm-Up Rule: Never skip this. At 45 your joints and connective tissue need 5 minutes to get synovial fluid moving. Cold training is how injuries happen. Total time: ~5 min.
Main Work
Start
Finish
Dumbbell Shoulder Press
4 sets × 10–12 reps
Seated or standing. Press from ear level up. Keep tension on delts throughout.
▶ YouTube Rest 75 sec
Start
Finish
Side Lateral Raise
4 sets × 15 reps
Raise to shoulder height only. Slow the negative. This creates width.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Alternating Front Raise
3 sets × 12 reps each
One arm at a time to shoulder height. Front delt. Slow and controlled.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Quadro Board — Shoulder/Pike Push-Up
PUSH-UP BOARD
3 sets × 10 reps
Red shoulder position on Quadro board. Pike your hips up high, press overhead angle. Excellent shoulder builder requiring zero equipment beyond the board.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Seated Rear Delt Fly
3 sets × 15 reps
Light weight, feel the squeeze behind the shoulder. Rear delts make shoulders look complete.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Arnold Press
3 sets × 10 reps
Rotate palms-facing-you at bottom to palms-forward at top. Hits all three delt heads.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
❄️ Cool-Down — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Child's Pose
COOL-DOWN
60 sec hold
Kneel, sit back on heels, arms forward on floor. Decompresses the spine and stretches the lats and lower back after any pulling day. Breathe deeply.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
COOL-DOWN
30 sec each side
Lunge position, drop back knee to floor, push hips forward. Hip flexors tighten from sitting — stretch them daily. Especially important after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Groin and Inner Thigh Stretch
COOL-DOWN
45 sec hold
Wide stance, reach forward or side bend. Stretches the adductors and groin. Important for hip mobility and recovery after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Plank Hold
COOL-DOWN
30–45 sec
End every session with a plank. Resets core stability, teaches proper bracing, and is active recovery for the spine after heavy lifting.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Cool-Down Rule: Static stretches after training (not before). Hold each stretch — don't bounce. This is when flexibility actually improves and when your nervous system downregulates. Total time: ~5 min.
Tip: Wide shoulders on a lean frame are the fastest visual upgrade you can make. Lateral raises with slow negatives are the key — not heavy loads.
Saturday
Abs + Full Body
GHD, reformer, and balance board all in the core section. End with heavy deadlift and KB swing. Sunday = full rest.
⚡ Warm-Up — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Arm Circles + Shoulder Rolls
WARM-UP
60 sec each direction
Big circles forward then backward. Loosens the shoulder capsule before any pressing or pulling. Never skip this at 45.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Torso Rotation
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Arms out to sides, rotate upper body left and right. Wakes up the thoracic spine and core. Critical before back and shoulder days.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Knee Circles + Hip Opener
WARM-UP
30 sec each direction
Hands on knees, circle outward then inward. Then hip circles. Gets blood into the joints before any lower body work.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Inchworm
WARM-UP
5–6 reps
Stand, fold forward, walk hands out to plank, walk feet to hands, stand. Full body activation — hamstrings, core, shoulders all in one movement.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Bodyweight Squat
WARM-UP
10 reps
Slow controlled squats, full depth. Activates glutes, quads, and warms the hips. Add a pause at the bottom.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Mountain Climbers
WARM-UP
20 reps (10 each leg)
Plank position, drive knees to chest alternating. Gets heart rate up and activates the core before training.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Warm-Up Rule: Never skip this. At 45 your joints and connective tissue need 5 minutes to get synovial fluid moving. Cold training is how injuries happen. Total time: ~5 min.
Core / Abs
Start
Finish
GHD Sit-Up
GHD
3 sets × 10–15 reps
Lock feet in Freak Athlete GHD, go all the way back past parallel, sit all the way up. Far superior to floor crunches. Works entire anterior chain.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Reformer Pike / Plank
REFORMER
3 sets × 10 reps
Feet on the reformer carriage in plank position, pike hips up by pulling carriage toward the foot bar. Incredible core and shoulder stability exercise. One of the best ab movements on the reformer.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Ab Roller Rollout
3 sets × 10–12 reps
Use your pro ab roller stand. Go as far as possible keeping lower back flat.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
Start
Finish
Balance Board Plank
BALANCE BOARD
3 sets × 30–45 sec
Place hands on the wobble balance board in plank position. The instability forces deep core activation — much harder than a floor plank.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
Start
Finish
Russian Twist with DB
3 sets × 20 reps (10 each)
Hold one DB, lean back 45°, rotate side to side. Obliques — defined waistline.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
Start
Finish
Flat Bench Leg Raise
3 sets × 15 reps
Lower back pressed flat the whole time. Slow the descent — lower abs fire hardest on way down.
▶ YouTube Rest 45 sec
Full Body Finishers
Start
Finish
Barbell Deadlift
3 sets × 6–8 reps
Full body king. Bar against legs, lock hips and knees at top. Heavy and controlled.
▶ YouTube Rest 90 sec
Start
Finish
Kettlebell Swing
3 sets × 20 reps
Hip-hinge power. Posterior chain and metabolic burn. Close the week strong.
▶ YouTube Rest 60 sec
❄️ Cool-Down — 5 Minutes (Every Day)
Start
Finish
Child's Pose
COOL-DOWN
60 sec hold
Kneel, sit back on heels, arms forward on floor. Decompresses the spine and stretches the lats and lower back after any pulling day. Breathe deeply.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
COOL-DOWN
30 sec each side
Lunge position, drop back knee to floor, push hips forward. Hip flexors tighten from sitting — stretch them daily. Especially important after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Groin and Inner Thigh Stretch
COOL-DOWN
45 sec hold
Wide stance, reach forward or side bend. Stretches the adductors and groin. Important for hip mobility and recovery after leg day.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Start
Finish
Plank Hold
COOL-DOWN
30–45 sec
End every session with a plank. Resets core stability, teaches proper bracing, and is active recovery for the spine after heavy lifting.
▶ YouTube Rest —
Cool-Down Rule: Static stretches after training (not before). Hold each stretch — don't bounce. This is when flexibility actually improves and when your nervous system downregulates. Total time: ~5 min.
Tip: The reformer pike is one of the hardest core exercises you can do — it requires total body tension. Start with knees bent on the carriage and work up to straight legs over several weeks.
6-Day Full Body Program · All Equipment Incorporated · Warm-Up & Cool-Down Every Day
Progressive overload every 2 weeks · Sunday = full rest
GHD = Freak Athlete · Reformer = Pilates Reformer · Board = Quadro Push-Up Board
